title: Service Technician / Engineer - Marine Electronics Equipment
description:
JOB SUMMARY

Carry out production, service, installation and commissioning of equipment and to ensure that jobs are carried out to meet the acceptance criteria as per technical/ installation manual. Identify and report quality problems related to product and service to Service Manager.

RESPONSIBILITIES

• Carry out production, service, installation and commissioning of equipment.

• Inform Superior of additional requirements from customers.

• Carry out receipt and inspection of customer supplied products.

• Ensure that jobs are carried out to meet the acceptance criteria as per technical manual and customers’ requirements.

• Provide interconnection cabling diagram for equipment sold to customers.

• Commissioning of equipment after installations.

• Identify and record quality issues related to products and services.

• Carry out other service/ technical duties as and delegated by Superior.

(For Engineer Level)

• Supervise technician in the production, servicing, installation and commissioning of equipment.

• Authorize the release of equipment to the customers after meeting specified standards in the service, repair, installation and commissioning work.

• Decide on the location for new installation work.

• Supervise of sub-contractors at work.

• Prepare and endorse service report.


KNOWLEDGE / SKILL REQUIREMENTS

• Reading comprehension of technical documents and work related documents in English.

• Possess good critical thinking and trouble-shooting skill.

• Able to communicate well, both in written and spoken English.

• Knowledge in Computers and Electronics, Telecommunication System.

• Knowledge and experience in Marine Electronics, Offshore industry is an advantage.


EDUCATION / EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS

• Minimum NTC/ITC in Electronics Engineering/ equivalent qualification with five (5) years working related experience.

• Diploma in Electronics Engineering with five (5) years related experience.


Working hours:
Mon to Fri, 9am to 6pm or 8am to 5pm
